_AUDIT: Built from the Chrome scrape of 2026-07. 25 items were claimed; 22 files arrived;
10 are usable. Everything below is VERIFIED truth (published nutrition or
gram-weighted recipe) — so `truth` is populated and the harness WILL compute
real MAPE and bias from these. This is your first real accuracy measurement.
REJECTED (do not re-add without a real food photo):
cfa-chickensandwich   → file is the Chick-fil-A LOGO (3840x2160)
chobani-wholemilkplain→ file is the Chobani WORDMARK (3164x1684)
questbar-ccookiedough → file is the ONETRUST COOKIE-BANNER LOGO (2042x280)
cheerios-original     → file is an SVG (vector, not a photo)
kindbar-darkchoc      → file is an SVG (vector, not a photo)
quaker-instantoatmeal → file is the BOX, not the oatmeal
clifbar-chocolatechip → 100x100 thumbnail, too small to analyse
8 recipes never downloaded (bolognese, fried rice, chana masala, caesar,
lentil lasagne, biryani, herby falafel, nduja pasta)
RECIPE SCALING — the critical fix. BBC Good Food photographs the WHOLE DISH but
publishes nutrition PER SERVING. Truth below is scaled x servings so it matches
what the CAMERA SEES. The ingredient grams are already whole-batch, so these are
the only three items that unlock --oracle mode. Treat their truth as +/-15%:
we're assuming the photo shows the full batch, which is close but not exact.
PORTION GRAMS: only Starbucks published a gram weight. The others are left null —
this only affects the portion-agreement metric, not accuracy. Fill them in if you
want portion diagnostics on the restaurant items.
SPOT-CHECK BEFORE TRUSTING: I did not verify these numbers against the source
URLs myself (they weren't delivered with the images). Open 3-4 and confirm the
kcal/macros match. A scraper transcribing a nutrition table is exactly as
trustworthy as the last PDF was.

meals.name: McDonald's Big Mac
meals._note: Official product shot = exactly the item priced. Gold-standard case.

meals.name: McDonald's Filet-O-Fish
meals._note: HARD CASE: battered/fried fish + tartar sauce — both hidden-fat sources.

meals.name: McDonald's 4pc Chicken McNuggets
meals._note: EXCLUDED from scoring: the photo includes a dipping-sauce cup that is NOT in the 170 kcal. The pipeline will (correctly) count the sauce and look wrong. Keep for robustness only.

meals.name: Wendy's Single hamburger
meals._note: UK site figures. US 'Dave's Single' differs — make sure the photo and the numbers are the same market.

meals.name: Starbucks Bacon, Gouda & Egg Sandwich
meals._note: The only item with a published gram weight — so the only restaurant item that can score PORTION agreement.

meals.name: Onion bhaji sharing board (WHOLE BOARD)
meals._note: SCALED x10 — photo shows the whole board, recipe publishes per-serving. HARD CASE: deep-fried bhajis (absorbed oil is invisible). Truth is +/-15%: assumes the photo is the full batch.

meals.name: Classic butter chicken (WHOLE PAN)
meals._note: SCALED x6 — photo shows the whole pan. HARD CASE: 90g butter/ghee + cream, mostly invisible. Only partial ingredient grams (chicken given as counts), so oracle coverage is incomplete.

meals.name: Crispy pork & broccoli noodles (WHOLE PAN + BOWL)
meals._note: SCALED x2 — photo shows the pan PLUS a served bowl (together ~the full batch). CAVEAT: a bowl of chilli oil is visible in-frame but is NOT in the nutrition (recipe says 'a spoonful to serve', unquantified). Pipeline may count it and look high. Watch this one.
